faq.label: Questions and answers about the application
faq.Qtech: What technologies were used to build the application?
faq.Atech: The application is built with TypeScript, React, and Vite. Below is a list of all libraries used.
faq.Qstorage: Where does the application store the entered and used data?
faq.Astorage: The application uses the JavaScript LocalStorage interface, which stores data in the browser's local storage. Currently, the application does not use a separate server and runs entirely as a frontend on the GH Pages service.
faq.Qlanguages: Is it possible to add new languages to the application?
faq.Alanguages: Yes, the application uses dynamic localization which allows new languages to be added relatively easily. The GH repository contains JSON files under src/i18n that hold key-value pairs for rendering. However, the current logic still requires a few additions to the code to make the language selectable.
faq.QdayPlanner: How does the daily nutrition page work?
faq.AdayPlanner1: The list on the right contains all meals and drinks stored in memory that the user has added. Above the list there is a console containing buttons for adding new rows, an input field for searching rows, and a dropdown menu for sorting by different nutritional values. Pressing the sort button again reverses the sorting order.
faq.AdayPlanner2: Each row contains a drag icon on the left for moving it, the row name, and a button for context-dependent deletion. If the row is in the memory list on the right, it is removed entirely from memory; if it is in the day's list on the left, it is only removed from the day and returned to the memory list. The row also contains a button that opens nutritional values per 100g/ml, the kcal total, and a numeric field for controlling the quantity. The total values depend on the quantity.
faq.AdayPlanner3: Rows are moved to the day's meals and drinks by dragging them from one list to another. After dragging, the daily nutritional totals are shown in the console located in the center of the page. The console also allows setting a limit for each nutritional value and shows how much of that limit is filled as a percentage. The limit can be enabled from a button located on the nutritional value row.
faq.AdayPlanner4: Below the console there is a list that displays the day's meals again, but sorts them according to the selected nutritional value and shows how large a portion of the total each meal or drink represents. The nutritional value is selected from the button group between the console and the list.
faq.Qmobile: Does the application work on mobile devices?
faq.Amobile: The currently released MVP version does not support mobile devices even though the application is responsive. This is due to the drag-and-drop feature, which may not work as expected on horizontal screens or touch controls. In the future, a simplified alternative control method is planned to enable use on mobile devices.
faq.Asecurity: Since all data is stored in the user's browser and no server is used, the data never leaves the device. This ensures that the information remains completely local and secure.
faq.QmultiDevice: Can I use the application on multiple devices simultaneously?
faq.AmultiDevice: Currently, the data is stored only in the browser's localStorage, so different devices do not share the same data. In the future, cloud synchronization may be considered so multiple devices could use the same data. With the current structure, it is also possible to use an export/import feature if needed.
faq.Qopensource: Is the source code of the site open?
faq.Aopensource: The source code of the site is open and can be found in the application's repository.
